column1: # templates/governance-map.tsv — the DETERMINISTIC governance-adoption pattern table (HERD-119).
# `herd init`'s opt-in adoption pass reads an OPTIONAL CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md (one source among many —
# never required), splits it into statements, and matches each against the PATTERNS below to propose a
# herd governance surface. Deterministic-first: this table is tried before any LLM fallback, so a
# known statement maps identically every run. Fail-soft: an unmatched statement is NOT an error (it
# flows to the style-convention heuristic or the gap report).
# Columns (TAB-separated):
#   pattern  — an egrep ERE, matched case-insensitively against a source statement. First row that
#              matches wins. Keep patterns specific enough to avoid false positives on prose.
#   surface  — the ENFORCEMENT SURFACE the rule renders to. Exactly one of:
#                config-key   → a MERGE-TIME .herd/config key (applied via `herd config set` on accept)
#                provisioning → session grounding: the REVIEW_CHECKLIST file / CONTEXT_PROVISION notes
#                hook         → a SESSION-TIME Claude Code settings.json hook. NOT rendered by this
#                               item — a sequenced follow-up ('Governance hook-rendering') consumes
#                               these rows; the `target` already carries the hook spec so that
#                               follow-up needs ZERO table rework.
#   target   — surface-specific:
#                config-key   → SPACE-separated KEY=VALUE pair(s); each VALUE is shell-safe and
#                               space-free (applied one `herd config set KEY VALUE` per pair).
#                provisioning → a provisioning lane token (review-checklist).
#                hook         → an opaque hook spec consumed by the follow-up (e.g. pre-action:run-checks).
#   label    — a short human description of the rule, shown in the interview proposal alongside the
#              QUOTED source sentence as evidence.
pattern
(human|manual|someone|a person|reviewer).{0,40}(review|sign.?off|approv).{0,40}(before|prior|ahead of).{0,20}(push|upload|merg)
column4: Human review required before changes are pushed/uploaded

column1: (review|sign.?off|approv).{0,30}(before|prior).{0,20}(push|upload)
column4: Changes reviewed before they are pushed

column1: (never|no|not|don.?t|do not).{0,25}auto.?.?merg
require.{0,30}(approval|sign.?off|review).{0,20}(before|to|prior).{0,15}merg
squash.?.?merg
rebase.?.?merg
delete.{0,20}(the )?(head |feature )?branch(es)?.{0,20}(after|on|once|when).{0,15}merg
column4: Delete the head branch after merge

column1: (open|create|submit).{0,25}(pr|prs|pull request).{0,15}(as|in).{0,10}draft|draft.{0,10}(pr|prs|pull request)
(never|no|not|don.?t|do not).{0,30}(co.?author|coauthor).{0,20}(claude|ai|assistant|bot|model)|no.?ai.?co.?author
column4: Never add an AI co-author trailer to commits

column1: (use|follow|adopt).{0,20}conventional.?.?commit
column4: Use Conventional Commits for commit messages

column1: (run|execute).{0,25}(the )?(lint|linter|format|prettier|test|tests|suite).{0,25}(before|prior|ahead).{0,15}(commit|committing|push|pushing|merge)
column4: Run checks (lint/test/format) before committing or pushing

column1: (format|reformat|run .*formatter|prettier).{0,25}(before|on).{0,15}(commit|committing|save|saving)
